Malaria.advice: Visit doctor immediately. Take prescribed antimalarial drugs. Use mosquito nets. Stay hydrated.

Dengue Fever.advice: Hospitalize immediately. Monitor platelet count daily. IV fluids. Papaya leaf juice helps. Avoid aspirin/ibuprofen.

Typhoid Fever.advice: Consult doctor urgently. Eat soft digestible food. Drink only boiled water. Complete full antibiotic course.

Tuberculosis.advice: Go to government hospital — TB treatment is FREE under RNTCP. Take full DOTS course (6 months). Cover mouth while coughing.

Cholera.advice: Drink ORS immediately. Hospitalize. Complete antibiotic course. Boil all drinking water. Strict hygiene.

Chikungunya.advice: Rest completely. Paracetamol for fever. Avoid aspirin. Anti-inflammatory for joints. Physiotherapy for joint pain.

Zika Virus.advice: Rest and drink fluids. Paracetamol for fever. Pregnant women must see doctor immediately. Use mosquito protection.

Leptospirosis.advice: Hospitalize immediately. IV antibiotics (Penicillin/Doxycycline). Avoid flooded water. Common after floods in India.

Japanese Encephalitis.advice: MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Hospitalize immediately. No specific antiviral — supportive care. Vaccination available — get it.

Rabies.advice: FATAL if untreated. After animal bite — wash wound 15 min with soap, go to hospital IMMEDIATELY for PEP vaccination.

Scrub Typhus.advice: See doctor immediately. Doxycycline is the treatment. Common in rural India. Avoid scrub vegetation.

Kala-Azar (Visceral Leishmaniasis).advice: Free treatment available at govt hospitals. Liposomal Amphotericin B. Do not delay. Endemic in Bihar, Jharkhand.

Filariasis.advice: Mass Drug Administration (MDA) available free. DEC + Albendazole. Lymphedema management. Govt elimination programme.

Common Cold.advice: Rest. Warm turmeric milk, ginger tea, steam inhalation. Avoid cold food. Honey+ginger helps. Usually resolves in 7 days.

Influenza (Flu).advice: Rest completely. Paracetamol for fever. Warm fluids. Antiviral (Oseltamivir) if severe. Annual flu vaccination recommended.

COVID-19.advice: Isolate immediately. Consult doctor. Monitor SpO2. Seek emergency care if SpO2 < 94%. Stay vaccinated and boosted.

Chickenpox.advice: Calamine lotion for itching. Cut nails short. Isolate from others. Acyclovir if severe. Vaccination prevents it.

Measles.advice: Hospitalize if severe. Vitamin A supplements. MMR vaccination prevents measles — ensure children are vaccinated.

Mumps.advice: Rest. Soft foods. Paracetamol for pain. MMR vaccine prevents mumps. Isolate from others for 5 days after swelling.

Hepatitis A.advice: Rest. Avoid alcohol completely. Light diet. Drink clean water only. Vaccination available — get it. Usually self-limiting.

Hepatitis B.advice: See hepatologist. Antiviral therapy (Tenofovir/Entecavir). Avoid alcohol. Vaccination available — 3 doses. Don't share needles.

Hepatitis C.advice: DAA therapy (Sofosbuvir) — 95% cure rate. Free under PMGSY in India. Avoid alcohol. Don't share needles.

Hepatitis E.advice: Hospitalize. Drink only boiled water. Very dangerous in pregnancy. Rest and supportive care. No specific antiviral.

HIV/AIDS.advice: ART therapy available FREE at govt hospitals. CD4 count monitoring. ICTC centres for free testing. Do not stigmatize.

Herpes Zoster (Shingles).advice: Antiviral (Acyclovir) within 72 hours. Calamine lotion. Pain management. Avoid contact with pregnant women/newborns.

Hand Foot Mouth Disease.advice: Supportive care. Cold fluids and soft food. Paracetamol for fever. Highly contagious — wash hands frequently.

Pneumonia.advice: Hospitalize if SpO2 low. Antibiotics (Amoxicillin/Azithromycin). Oxygen therapy if needed. Complete full antibiotic course.

Meningitis.advice: MEDICAL EMERGENCY — call 108 immediately. IV antibiotics. Can be fatal within hours. Do not delay.

Urinary Tract Infection.advice: Drink 3L water daily. Antibiotics (Nitrofurantoin/Trimethoprim). Complete course. Maintain hygiene. Cranberry juice helps.

Typhus.advice: Doxycycline antibiotic. Hospitalize if severe. Louse/flea control. Common in overcrowded areas.

Diphtheria.advice: Antitoxin + antibiotics immediately. Hospitalize. DPT vaccination prevents it — ensure children are vaccinated.

Whooping Cough (Pertussis).advice: Azithromycin antibiotic. Hospitalize infants. DPT vaccination prevents it. Isolate patient. Very contagious.

Tetanus.advice: EMERGENCY — hospitalize immediately. TT injection after any wound. TDaP vaccine prevents tetanus. Clean all wounds.

Leprosy.advice: FREE MDT treatment at govt hospitals. 6-12 months treatment. Early detection prevents disability. No stigma — it is curable.

Type 2 Diabetes.advice: Control sugar diet. Exercise 30 min daily. Metformin as prescribed. Monitor HbA1c every 3 months. Avoid sugar/rice excess.

Type 1 Diabetes.advice: Insulin therapy mandatory. Monitor blood glucose 4x daily. Carry glucose tablets always. Emergency: glucagon injection.

Hypertension.advice: DASH diet — low salt, fruits, vegetables. Exercise daily. Take prescribed antihypertensives. Monitor BP daily at home.

Heart Attack (MI).advice: CALL 108 IMMEDIATELY. Chew 325mg aspirin if available. Do NOT drive. Time is muscle — every minute counts.

Heart Failure.advice: Restrict fluid and salt. Diuretics + ACE inhibitors as prescribed. Weigh daily. No strenuous activity. Cardiac rehab.

Stroke.advice: CALL 108 IMMEDIATELY — FAST: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call. Thrombolysis within 4.5 hrs.

Coronary Artery Disease.advice: Statins + aspirin as prescribed. Diet modification. Cardiac rehab. Angioplasty if required. Stop smoking immediately.

Bronchial Asthma.advice: Carry salbutamol inhaler always. Avoid dust/smoke/allergens. Use spacer with inhaler. Step up therapy if uncontrolled.

COPD.advice: Stop smoking IMMEDIATELY. Bronchodilators (Tiotropium). Pulmonary rehab. Flu + pneumococcal vaccines. Oxygen if needed.

Hypothyroidism.advice: Levothyroxine daily on empty stomach. TSH test every 6 months. Avoid soy and high-fiber near medication time.

Hyperthyroidism.advice: Antithyroid drugs (Methimazole/PTU). Beta-blockers for symptoms. Radioiodine or surgery if medical therapy fails.

Anemia.advice: Iron tablets (ferrous sulfate) with vitamin C. Iron-rich foods: spinach, jaggery, dates, beans. Treat underlying cause.

Sickle Cell Disease.advice: Hydroxyurea reduces crises. Pain management during crisis. Folic acid daily. Vaccinations important. Genetic counseling.

Gastroenteritis.advice: ORS every 30 minutes. Zinc tablets for children. BRAT diet (Banana, Rice, Apple, Toast). See doctor if continues > 2 days.

Food Poisoning.advice: ORS and fluids. No solid food for 6 hours. BRAT diet. See doctor if vomiting blood or diarrhea for > 2 days.

GERD / Acid Reflux.advice: Small frequent meals. Avoid spicy/oily/acidic food. Don't lie down after eating. Elevate bed head. PPI (Omeprazole) if needed.

Peptic Ulcer.advice: PPI + H.pylori eradication (triple therapy). Avoid NSAIDs, alcohol, spicy food. Eat regular small meals.

Appendicitis.advice: SURGICAL EMERGENCY. Go to hospital immediately. Do NOT take painkillers before diagnosis. Appendectomy required.

Irritable Bowel Syndrome.advice: High fiber diet. Identify trigger foods. Stress management. Probiotics. Antispasmodics for pain. Lifestyle changes key.

Piles (Hemorrhoids).advice: High fiber diet. Sitz bath. Hemorrhoid creams. Avoid straining. Surgery (rubber band ligation) for severe cases.

Celiac Disease.advice: Strict gluten-free diet for LIFE. Avoid wheat, barley, rye. Nutritional supplements. Regular follow-up with gastroenterologist.

Jaundice.advice: Identify and treat underlying cause. Avoid alcohol. Light diet. Rest. Drink sugarcane juice. See doctor urgently.

Liver Cirrhosis.advice: Stop alcohol completely. Low salt diet. Diuretics for fluid. Lactulose for confusion. Transplant for end-stage.

Alcoholic Hepatitis.advice: Stop alcohol IMMEDIATELY. Hospitalize if severe. Corticosteroids if indicated. Nutritional support. De-addiction counseling.

Bronchitis.advice: Rest. Warm fluids. Steam inhalation. Bronchodilators if needed. Antibiotics only if bacterial. Stop smoking.

Pleurisy.advice: Hospitalize. NSAIDs for pain. Treat underlying cause. Thoracentesis if fluid present. Rest and monitoring.

Pulmonary Embolism.advice: EMERGENCY — call 108. Anticoagulation therapy (Heparin). Thrombolytics if severe. Can be fatal if untreated.

Epilepsy.advice: Antiepileptic drugs (Valproate/Levetiracetam). Never miss dose. No driving during uncontrolled seizures. Safety precautions.

Migraine.advice: Dark quiet room. Cold compress. Triptans for acute attack. Preventive: Topiramate/Propranolol. Identify triggers.

Alzheimer's Disease.advice: Cholinesterase inhibitors (Donepezil). Caregiver support essential. Safe home environment. Memory activities. No cure yet.

Parkinson's Disease.advice: Levodopa/Carbidopa therapy. Physiotherapy and occupational therapy. Exercise improves outcomes. Deep brain stimulation for severe cases.

Vertigo.advice: Epley maneuver for BPPV. Vestibular sedatives (Betahistine). Vestibular rehab exercises. Avoid sudden head movements.

Cervical Spondylosis.advice: Physiotherapy. Cervical collar for acute pain. NSAIDs. Neck exercises. Surgery if neurological deficit worsens.

Depression.advice: Antidepressants (SSRIs) + psychotherapy. iCall/Vandrevala Foundation helpline: 1860-2662-345. Exercise helps. Do not isolate.

Anxiety Disorder.advice: CBT therapy. SSRIs/SNRIs. Breathing exercises. Mindfulness. Avoid caffeine. Exercise regularly. Helpline: 1800-599-0019.

Rheumatoid Arthritis.advice: DMARDs (Methotrexate). NSAIDs for pain. Physiotherapy. Low-impact exercise. Regular rheumatologist follow-up.

Osteoarthritis.advice: Weight loss reduces stress on joints. Physiotherapy. Paracetamol/NSAIDs. Knee replacement for severe cases.

Gout.advice: Colchicine/NSAIDs for acute attack. Allopurinol for prevention. Avoid red meat, alcohol, purine-rich foods. Drink lots of water.

Osteoporosis.advice: Calcium + Vitamin D3 supplements. Bisphosphonates (Alendronate). Weight-bearing exercise. Fall prevention. Bone density scan.

Chronic Kidney Disease.advice: Low protein, low potassium diet. Strict BP control. Dialysis if GFR < 15. Avoid NSAIDs/contrast dye. Transplant option.

Kidney Stones.advice: Drink 3-4 litres water daily. IV pain relief for acute pain. ESWL/ureteroscopy for stones > 5mm. Low oxalate diet.

Nephrotic Syndrome.advice: Prednisolone as prescribed. Low salt, moderate protein diet. Diuretics for edema. Monitor urine protein regularly.

Psoriasis.advice: Topical corticosteroids. Moisturize regularly. Avoid triggers: stress, infections. Biologics for severe cases. Phototherapy.

Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis).advice: Moisturize 2x daily. Topical steroids for flares. Avoid triggers: soaps, detergents, pet dander. Antihistamines for itch.

Ringworm (Tinea).advice: Antifungal cream (Clotrimazole/Fluconazole). Keep area dry. Don't share towels/combs. Oral antifungals for severe cases.

Scabies.advice: Permethrin 5% cream all over body. All household members treated simultaneously. Wash all clothes/bedding in hot water.

Acne.advice: Benzoyl peroxide/Salicylic acid wash. Don't pop pimples. Low GI diet. Topical retinoids. Isotretinoin for severe cystic acne.

Vitiligo.advice: Topical steroids / tacrolimus. Phototherapy (PUVA/NB-UVB). Sun protection. Cosmetic cover. Psychological support important.

Fungal Infection.advice: Antifungal cream (Clotrimazole/Miconazole). Keep skin dry. Avoid tight clothing. Oral antifungals (Fluconazole) if severe.

Impetigo.advice: Topical mupirocin. Oral antibiotics if widespread. Keep area clean. Highly contagious — don't share towels.

Conjunctivitis (Pink Eye).advice: Antibiotic eye drops (Ciprofloxacin). Cool compress. Don't rub eyes. Very contagious — wash hands frequently.

Cataract.advice: Surgery (phacoemulsification) is the only treatment. FREE at govt hospitals under NPCB. Don't delay — can cause blindness.

Glaucoma.advice: Eye drops (Timolol/Latanoprost) daily. Laser/surgery if eye drops fail. Regular IOP monitoring. Cannot reverse damage.

Diabetic Retinopathy.advice: Strict blood sugar control. Annual retinal exam for all diabetics. Laser photocoagulation. Anti-VEGF injections. Early detection saves sight.

Otitis Media (Ear Infection).advice: Antibiotics (Amoxicillin) for bacterial. Analgesics for pain. Avoid water in ear. Follow up after treatment.

Sinusitis.advice: Steam inhalation. Saline nasal spray. Decongestants. Antibiotics for bacterial sinusitis > 10 days. FESS surgery for chronic.

Tonsillitis.advice: Antibiotics (Penicillin/Amoxicillin) for bacterial. Warm salt gargle. Paracetamol for pain. Tonsillectomy for recurrent cases.

Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).advice: Weight loss (5-10%) improves symptoms significantly. Metformin. OCP for menstrual regulation. Inositol supplement. Exercise.

Endometriosis.advice: NSAIDs for pain. Hormonal therapy (OCP/GnRH). Laparoscopic surgery. Fertility treatment if needed. Manage with support.

Preeclampsia.advice: OBSTETRIC EMERGENCY. Hospitalize immediately. Magnesium sulfate for seizure prevention. Delivery may be needed. Monitor baby.

Childhood Malnutrition.advice: CMAM programme at PHC. Ready-to-use therapeutic food (RUTF). Treat infections. Micronutrient supplements. NRC admission if SAM.

Rotavirus Diarrhea.advice: ORS immediately. Zinc 20mg for 14 days. Breastfeed continuously. Hospitalize if severely dehydrated. Rotavirus vaccine prevents it.

Kawasaki Disease.advice: IVIG + Aspirin — start immediately. Echocardiogram mandatory. Hospitalize. Can cause coronary aneurysm if untreated.

Oral Cancer.advice: See oncologist urgently. Stop tobacco/betel nut/alcohol IMMEDIATELY. Biopsy for diagnosis. Surgery+radiation. Early detection = high survival.

Cervical Cancer.advice: HPV vaccination prevents it. Pap smear every 3 years. VIA screening at PHC is FREE. Early detection is curable. No stigma.

Breast Cancer.advice: Self-exam monthly. Mammogram after 40. PMNL scheme for free treatment. Surgery+chemo+radiation. Early stage = 95% survival.

Dehydration.advice: ORS solution. Coconut water. Drink 3L water daily. Avoid sugary drinks. IV fluids if severe dehydration. Seek shade.

Sunstroke / Heat Stroke.advice: Move to cool place immediately. Cold wet cloth on body. Cold water to drink. Call 108 if unconscious. Fan continuously.

Varicose Veins.advice: Compression stockings. Elevate legs. Exercise regularly. Avoid prolonged standing. Sclerotherapy/surgery for severe cases.

Allergic Rhinitis.advice: Identify allergens (dust, pollen, pet dander). Antihistamines (Cetirizine). Nasal corticosteroid spray. Allergen immunotherapy.

Drug Reaction / Allergy.advice: Stop the offending drug IMMEDIATELY. Antihistamines + corticosteroids. Call 108 if anaphylaxis. Carry allergy card.

Insomnia.advice: Fixed sleep schedule. No screens 1 hour before bed. No caffeine after 4pm. CBT-I therapy most effective. Melatonin for short-term.

Dengue Shock Syndrome.advice: ICU ADMISSION REQUIRED IMMEDIATELY. IV fluid resuscitation. Platelet transfusion. Intensive monitoring. CALL 108 NOW.

Hypoglycemia.advice: 15-15 rule: 15g fast carbs (glucose tab/juice), recheck after 15 min. If unconscious: glucagon injection. Then eat a meal.

Worm Infestation.advice: Albendazole 400mg single dose. All family members treat together. Wash hands before eating. Cook food well. Clean water.
